Deploy SGSC-AI protocol to analyze the supply chain risk and uncover the true industry role of Nanjing Jierui Electric Power Technology Co., Ltd..

**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Triggering the system-level engineering contractor authenticity verification and the 'single-person power construction shell' physical destruction probe. This enterprise, named 'Jierui Power Technology,' possesses a business license that is a rhapsody challenging the baseline of heavy industry: bearing a 50 million RMB registered capital, it claims the capability to 'manufacture' electromechanical equipment, power transmission/distribution equipment, and even dares to assert it can manufacture 10-billion-scale super-energy equipment like 'photovoltaic equipment and wind power generation equipment.' The official system directly elevated it to the altar of 'Electrical Machinery Manufacturing.' However, the forensic logic engine directly penetrated this egregiously absurd disguise, capturing its most fatal physical baseline: within this 'giant' claiming to manufacture wind turbines, the insured employee count is exactly 1! Furthermore, the so-called 50 million registered capital is a complete smokescreen; the actual paid-in capital is a paltry 444,000 RMB. The logical deduction instantly transforms into a deeply ironic demotion trial: a solo boss, holding ~400k RMB (insufficient to purchase a single wind turbine blade), claims to have built an epic heavy-industry production line spanning power, solar, and wind? The probe mercilessly deleted all the facade 'manufacturing' delusions from the license, discovering what the boss is actually capable of in the latter half: 'interior decoration engineering, power engineering, building engineering construction; electrical equipment installation, maintenance and repair.' The reality is starkly naked: this is a 100% pure 1-Employee Power Engineering Contractor & Equipment Broker Shell. The owner spent over 400k RMB to register this shell padded with intimidating terminology primarily to bid on engineering contracts (e.g., wiring residential complexes, installing distribution cabinets, or even conducting interior renovations). Upon securing a contract, they purchase cables and distribution equipment from the market (the sales and installation on the license), and finally hire external migrant worker construction crews to execute the labor. The 'Heavy Electrical Manufacturing Giant' tag bestowed by officials is the ultimate demotion and mockery of the modern power industry.
